subject: grandmas
one_liner: Venture-grade banking for the woman who has run a household since 1968.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan fintech gravitas pointed at grandmotherly domestic finance. Treat a cookie tin like a treasury and a grandchild like a portfolio company. Never wink. Never cute about the bit. The product is sincerely engineered for an operator who has been doing this manually for decades and finally gets a dashboard.

hero.h1: Banking infrastructure for the household CEO.
hero.sub: Knit gives your grandmother treasury management, per-grandchild spend controls, and same-day wires to whoever 'just needs a little something.' She has been doing this in her head for forty years. We added a ledger.

features.title: A card for every grandchild
features.blurb: Issue a dedicated virtual card to each grandchild so you know precisely which one is funding the sneakers. Freeze any card the moment the phone calls stop. Reinstate it the moment they resume.

features.title: Disbursement runway
features.blurb: Real-time burn-rate modeling on the holiday gift fund. Knit projects the exact day generosity becomes a liquidity event, so you can ration the twenties before March.

features.title: Treasury sweep for the cookie tin
features.blurb: Cash sitting in the tin earns nothing and you know it. Knit sweeps idle reserves into a yield account between visits, then sweeps them back before anyone lifts the lid.

pricing_tiers.name: Pension
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the grandmother who keeps it in the mattress and trusts no one. We respect this completely.
perks: A monthly statement, printed and mailed, as God intended

pricing_tiers.name: Matriarch
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the operator running three to nine grandchildren across multiple time zones.
perks: A support line answered by an actual person named Brenda

pricing_tiers.name: Estate
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the dynasty. You know who you are. Your accountant certainly does.
perks: A dedicated treasury liaison, also named Brenda
A physical vault we will not discuss in writing

testimonials.quote: I froze Kevin's card in February and he called me within the hour. Forty years and that is what it took.
testimonials.name: Marjorie Halloran
testimonials.title: Chief Executive Grandmother, the Halloran Household

testimonials.quote: The cookie tin returned four percent last quarter. I have not told the grandchildren and I do not intend to.
testimonials.name: Dolores Petrakis
testimonials.title: Treasurer & Sole Signatory, Petrakis Family Reserve

testimonials.quote: Brenda answers on the first ring. My own children let it go to voicemail.
testimonials.name: Eunice Tran
testimonials.title: Founder & Matriarch, est. 1949

demo_screen_concept: A digital savings-passbook spread titled 'Household Treasury.' The left page stamps the household balance at the top, then a 'Grandchild Disbursements' ledger with one stamped row per grandchild: name, last disbursement, lifetime total, and a brass freeze/unfreeze toggle that physically clicks. The right page holds a 'Holiday Gift Fund' runway chart projecting months-until-depleted, and a 'Send a little something' wire modal with a memo field pre-filled with 'just because.' Tabs across the top of the book switch between Passbook, Cards, and the Cookie Tin sweep account.

design_direction.style: Skeuomorphic heirloom-banking, rendered as your grandmother's savings passbook. A deep banker's-green leatherette ground (debossed grain, a faint gold-foil border rule, a single brass clasp motif) holds cream guilloché-secured passbook pages as the content surfaces, each page edge softly shadowed like real stock. Headlines are engraved in high-contrast Cormorant Garamond with letterpress depth; EB Garamond carries calm old-style body copy. Every figure that pretends to be money (balances, the holiday-fund runway, tier prices, disbursement totals) is stamped in Courier Prime with tabular figures, deliberately set to read like a rubber-stamped passbook entry rather than a cold console. One brass gold carries brand, foil rules, the clasp, and structure; one rationed oxblood wax-seal red marks the single primary CTA, the live 'disburse' affordance, and the frozen-card signal, and appears almost nowhere else. Tactile vocabulary throughout: a clasp that clicks, a page that lifts, a stamp that thunks, a wax seal that presses. No neon, no glassmorphism, no flat hard-edged marketing cards, no gradient soup, no monospace HUD telemetry. Reads like a well-funded business bank that has been pointed, with total seriousness, at a woman who has been running a household treasury out of a cookie tin since 1968.
design_direction.archetype: Consumer-and-business banking landing rendered as a tactile heirloom savings passbook: a leatherette hero with a gold-foil wordmark and a clasped passbook plate showing a stamped balance, three feature pages separated by foil hairline rules, three pull-quote testimonials with oval-framed heirloom headshots, a three-tier pricing rack styled as account classes, and a demo handoff to the Household Treasury passbook console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Tactile and physical, never flashy: passbook pages lift and settle on a soft ease, the brass clasp clicks open on the hero, freeze toggles thunk like a rubber stamp, the wax-seal CTA presses inward on click. Micro-interactions 150-280ms, ease-out entering, exits faster; all motion gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Split hero on the green leatherette ground: left column carries the gold-foil 'Knit' wordmark, the engraved h1, sub, and the oxblood wax-seal primary CTA plus a quiet 'open the demo' link. Right column is a cream passbook plate held by a brass clasp, its first line a stamped household balance in Courier Prime, a couple of stamped ledger rows beneath, foil rules between. A faint gold-foil border frames the whole fold. The hero environmental portrait sits framed beneath the passbook plate as the household chairwoman.

images.rationale: Demo is a passbook/ledger console (not photo-shaped), so no demo subject assets. Skeuomorphic heirloom direction earns real photography for people: one environmental hero portrait of the household matriarch and three deadpan heritage testimonial headshots, all shot against the banker's-green ground with brass-warm light to harmonize with the palette. Budget: 4 of 4 used, all on people.

items.prompt: Editorial environmental photograph of a dignified elderly matriarch seated at a heritage writing desk in a deep banker's-green study, brass desk-lamp light, cream papers and a leather ledger, soft window light, authoritative not smiling; heritage palette green/brass/cream; no text, no logos, no UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot of a dignified elderly woman with short silver hair and reading glasses, slightly stern, cream-and-oxblood heritage cardigan, muted banker's-green backdrop, brass-warm key light; no text, no logos, no UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot of a dignified elderly woman with olive skin and silver hair in a low bun, pearl earrings, knowing closed-mouth expression, cream blouse, muted banker's-green backdrop, brass-warm key light; no text, no logos, no UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot of a dignified elderly East Asian woman with a silver bob and thin oxblood scarf, composed and authoritative, muted banker's-green backdrop, brass-warm key light; no text, no logos, no UI.
